stem: When does a session (or batch) actually start — that is, when does its maximum TTL timer begin counting?

options.text: When its first job begins execution.
The moment you construct the `Session`/`Batch` object.
When you exit the context manager.

explanation.correct: A session or batch starts when its first job begins execution; that is when the maximum TTL timer starts. (Note: if you open a session but submit no jobs for 30 minutes, it closes automatically.)

distractors.B: Merely constructing the object does not start the clock — the first job entering execution does.
distractors.C: Exiting the context manager closes the session/batch; it does not start the TTL timer.
distractors.D: Creating the service only authenticates you; it is unrelated to when a session's timer begins.
